chunks.page_summary: This page provides a list of measurement parameters output by Rheonics sensors, along with their corresponding indices for field device access.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Parameter List Access for Field Devices

Modified on: Tue, 20 May, 2025 at 5:40 PM

Rheonics sensors output measurements referred to as parameters, starting at index 0. The output for each parameter is listed below.

Table 1. Parameters List

Index | Parameter
--- | ---
0 | viscosity median / 5 measurements
1 | density median / 5 measurements
2 | temperature median / 5 measurements
3 | Kinematic Viscosity
4 | density average / 5 measurements
5 | V - viscosity raw
6 | D - density raw
7 | T - temperature raw
8 | f - resonant frequency (Hz)
9 | fc - compensated resonant frequency (Hz)
10 | df - damping (Hz)
11 | Tc - Coil temperature
12 | viscosity median and last good
13 | density median and last good
14 | Displays of mapped value from Modbus register 512
key_facts: Rheonics sensors output measurements are referred to as parameters.
The parameters are indexed starting from 0.
The document was modified on Tuesday, May 20, 2025, at 5:40 PM.
Parameter 14 displays a mapped value from Modbus register 512.

chunks.page_summary: This page displays a table listing indexed parameters related to sensor measurements, calculated values from various models, and Modbus register mappings.
chunks.extracted_text:
Index | Parameter
15 | Displays of mapped value from Modbus register 514
16 | Displays of mapped value from Modbus register 516
17 | Te - Estimated Temperature (from internal temperature Algorithm)
18 | Tp - Temperature from PT1000 sensor present in sensor
19 | Calculated parameter from viscosity model
20 | Calculated parameter from density model
21 | Calculated parameter from concentration model
22 | Sensor cleanliness ratio
key_facts: Parameters 15 and 16 display mapped values retrieved from Modbus registers 514 and 516, respectively.
Parameter 17, 'Te', represents an estimated temperature calculated using an internal temperature algorithm.
Parameter 18, 'Tp', indicates the temperature measured by an integrated PT1000 sensor.
The device can provide calculated parameters derived from viscosity, density, and concentration models.
Parameter 22 provides a sensor cleanliness ratio.
